title: Contract key collision from missing lookupByKey before create
source_title: DAML Integration Pattern - Contract Key Uniqueness
description: The TokenFactory's Mint choice creates a Token contract with a key (issuer, symbol) without first checking if a token with that key already exists. DAML enforces key uniqueness at runtime: if a duplicate key is created, the transaction aborts. This causes a confusing runtime error instead of a graceful handling of the 'already exists' case.
recommendation: Always use lookupByKey before creating a keyed contract. If the key already exists, either update the existing contract or return a meaningful error.

oracle.description: Before creating a keyed contract, verify no active contract with the same key exists.
oracle.static_signal: create of keyed template without preceding lookupByKey/fetchByKey for the same key expression
oracle.symbolic_property: forall k. creates_keyed(choice, k) => preceded_by(lookupByKey(k)) || preceded_by(fetchByKey(k))

notes: Very common DAML developer mistake. Contract key uniqueness is enforced at runtime, making this a subtle correctness issue.
